open_known_gaps.summary: Uncapped per-page render loops with no page-count cap or processing timeout can exhaust client browser memory on a high-page-count PDF within the 50MB limit. Two sites: viewer.js loadDocument/renderAll (lines ~34-84) and thumbnails.js renderThumbnails (line ~161, loops 1..total). Client-side only, no server impact; thumbnails.js is the milder of the two (sequential, render-token-guarded, small bounded canvases). Fix is a developer task (shared page-count cap at the viewer.js load boundary). Documented in docs/security-guide.md.

notes: Clean review. New code this tick: tab-nav.js (WAI-ARIA tablist keyboard nav — pure focus/tabindex management, activates via existing tab.click() path, keydown handler guards ctrl/meta/alt; no innerHTML/eval/fetch/.src), thumbnails.js active-page tracking (TASK-312: self-contained IntersectionObserver, content via createElement/textContent, innerHTML only as container clear), and app.js wiring (no user content). DOM sink sweep across all js/: every innerHTML occurrence is a container clear (= ''), zero eval/Function/document.write, zero fetch/XHR/.src assignments, zero inline on* handlers. upload.js validation boundary unchanged. Live headers confirm full CSP + 4 security headers; sensitive paths (.git/.bak/traversal/.sh/.md) all 403; .mjs MIME text/javascript + nosniff; nginx -t valid; 0 failed SSH auths in 24h; no secrets in web JS (grep hits are render-token variables, not credentials). Only open item is SEC-002 (low, client-side PDF-bomb DoS via uncapped per-page render loops in viewer.js and thumbnails.js) — tracked for developer in docs/security-guide.md, not security's to implement.
